records.ety: [L. tripus, -odis, Gr. <?/; <?/ (see Tri-) + <?/, <?/, foot. See Foot, and cf. Tripos, Trivet.]

defs: Any utensil or vessel, as a stool, table, altar, caldron, etc., supported on three feet.
notes: ☞ On such, a stool, in the temple of Apollo at Delphi, the Pythian priestess sat while giving responses to those consulting the Delphic oracle.

defs: A three-legged frame or stand, usually jointed at top, for supporting a theodolite, compass, telescope, camera, or other instrument.
